title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: make ? Data dictionaries (referred “dictionaries” ) often developed outside computing session stored static file. Dictionaries helpful large projects many outputs, providing central resource consistent accurate annotation tables figures. However, process making using dictionaries tedious, perinary developed reduce friction. make dictionaries, two primary classes functions: get: retrieve meta data dictionary set: modify meta data dictionary use dictionary, three classes functions: translate: modify names category labels append: add rows /columns data frame index: order rows data frame get/set functions applied dictionary, translate append functions members DataDictionary class, meaning called dictionary object.

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: can install development version perinary GitHub : Important: update perinary, sure re-create existing dictionaries workflows newly installed perinary package. perinary uses R6, attaches functions objects, dictionaries made using older versions perinary contain functions incompatible newer versions perinary.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("perisphere-rwe/perinary")

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: Data dictionaries help organize meta data variables, storing information relevant tables figures easily stored data frames. data dictionary useful complex projects many variables /many expected outputs, particularly consistency outputs goal. two ways initialize dictionary. can build using NumericVariable NominalVariable objects (shown , see vignette), can tedious lot variables. cases, ’ll want use as_data_dictionary() create starter dictionary given dataset.
code: library(perinary) library(tidyverse) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.3 #> Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2 library(palmerpenguins)  data_peng <- penguins %>%    select(species, sex, body_mass_g, bill_length_mm, bill_depth_mm)  dd_peng <- as_data_dictionary(data_peng)  dd_peng #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name           type    label description units divby_modeling category_levels  #>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr> <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>            #> 1 species        Nominal none  none        none  none           Adelie, Chinstr… #> 2 sex            Nominal none  none        none  none           female and male  #> 3 body_mass_g    Numeric none  none        none  none           none             #> 4 bill_length_mm Numeric none  none        none  none           none             #> 5 bill_depth_mm  Numeric none  none        none  none           none             #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: Let’s honest, dictionaries annoying write. perinary aims make less annoying get_unknowns(). function tells us relevant information missing dictionary. set as_request = TRUE, bullet point list returned R console. text intended help start discussion filling gaps. want get straight filling unknowns, specify as_code = TRUE: get functions include get_term_key(): returns tibble links nominal variable categories modeling terms. helpful want incorporate meta information standard output modeling functions. get_dictionary(): returns tibble containing raw dictionary meta data. helpful want apply meta data non-standard way. general get functions exported DataDictionary class
code: get_unknowns(dd_peng, as_request = TRUE) #> A label to use for this variable in reports: #>  #>   - species = ? #>   - sex = ? #>   - body_mass_g = ? #>   - bill_length_mm = ? #>   - bill_depth_mm = ? #>  #> Category labels for this variable (labels are shown in reports): #>  #>   - species: Adelie = ?;  Chinstrap = ?;  Gentoo = ? #>   - sex: female = ?;  male = ? #>  #> Variable units (e.g., age in years): #>  #>   - body_mass_g = ? #>   - bill_length_mm = ? #>   - bill_depth_mm = ? get_unknowns(dd_peng, as_code = TRUE) #> set_labels(species  = "", #>            sex  = "", #>            body_mass_g  = "", #>            bill_length_mm  = "", #>            bill_depth_mm  = "") |>  #> set_category_labels(species = c(Adelie = "", #>                                 Chinstrap = "", #>                                 Gentoo = ""), #>                     sex = c(female = "", #>                             male = "")) |>  #> set_units(body_mass_g  = "", #>           bill_length_mm  = "", #>           bill_depth_mm  = "") get_term_key(dd_peng) #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>   name    level     label     reference category_type term             #>   <chr>   <chr>     <chr>     <lgl>     <chr>         <chr>            #> 1 species Adelie    Adelie    TRUE      levels        speciesAdelie    #> 2 species Chinstrap Chinstrap FALSE     levels        speciesChinstrap #> 3 species Gentoo    Gentoo    FALSE     levels        speciesGentoo    #> 4 sex     female    female    TRUE      levels        sexfemale        #> 5 sex     male      male      FALSE     levels        sexmale get_dictionary(dd_peng) #> # A tibble: 5 × 7 #>   name    label description units divby_modeling category_levels category_labels #>   <chr>   <chr> <chr>       <chr>          <dbl> <named list>    <named list>    #> 1 species <NA>  <NA>        <NA>              NA <chr [3]>       <chr [3]>       #> 2 sex     <NA>  <NA>        <NA>              NA <chr [2]>       <chr [2]>       #> 3 body_m… <NA>  <NA>        <NA>              NA <NULL>          <NULL>          #> 4 bill_l… <NA>  <NA>        <NA>              NA <NULL>          <NULL>          #> 5 bill_d… <NA>  <NA>        <NA>              NA <NULL>          <NULL>

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: perinary’s family set functions provide interface modify dictionary’s meta data.
code: dd_peng <- dd_peng %>%    set_labels(species = "Species",              sex = "Sex",              body_mass_g = "Body mass",              bill_length_mm = "Bill length",              bill_depth_mm = "Bill depth") %>%    set_units(bill_length_mm = "mm",             bill_depth_mm = "mm",             body_mass_g = "grams") %>%    set_divby_modeling(bill_length_mm = 5,                      bill_depth_mm = 5)

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: identifier variable uniquely defines sampling units data set. identifier variable can cause disruption data dictionary treated like factor. example, don’t want supply labels level identifier, don’t want summarize data level identifier. demonstrate problem identifiers can pose, ’s happened call get_unknowns() unmanaged identifier variable dictionary. issue don’t want need supply category labels penguin_id, isn’t helpful included list unknowns. fix designate penguin_id (generally identifier variables) identifier variable, take care potential downstream issues.

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: Modify category labels nominal variables set_category_labels(): Modify category order nominal variables set_category_order(): Modify variable order variable set_variable_order(). impacts order variables appear calling index_terms() (next section)
code: dd_peng <- dd_peng %>%    set_category_labels(sex = c(female = "Female penguins",                                male = "Male penguins"))  dd_peng #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name           type    label  description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr>  <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 species        Nominal Speci… none        none  none           Adelie, Chinst… #> 2 sex            Nominal Sex    none        none  none           female and male #> 3 body_mass_g    Numeric Body … none        grams none           none            #> 4 bill_length_mm Numeric Bill … none        mm    5              none            #> 5 bill_depth_mm  Numeric Bill … none        mm    5              none            #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr> # moves the specified category or categories to the front, putting the  # remaining categories behind with the same relative order to each other  dd_peng <- dd_peng %>%    set_category_order(sex = c("male"),                      species = c("Chinstrap"))  dd_peng #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name           type    label  description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr>  <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 species        Nominal Speci… none        none  none           Chinstrap, Ade… #> 2 sex            Nominal Sex    none        none  none           male and female #> 3 body_mass_g    Numeric Body … none        grams none           none            #> 4 bill_length_mm Numeric Bill … none        mm    5              none            #> 5 bill_depth_mm  Numeric Bill … none        mm    5              none            #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr> # moves the specified category or categories to the front, putting the  # remaining categories behind with the same relative order to each other  dd_peng <- dd_peng %>%    set_variable_order(species, .before = 1)  dd_peng #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name           type    label  description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr>          <chr>   <chr>  <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 species        Nominal Speci… none        none  none           Chinstrap, Ade… #> 2 sex            Nominal Sex    none        none  none           male and female #> 3 body_mass_g    Numeric Body … none        grams none           none            #> 4 bill_length_mm Numeric Bill … none        mm    5              none            #> 5 bill_depth_mm  Numeric Bill … none        mm    5              none            #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: translate function family includes translate_categories(), translate_names(), translate_data(). functions bridge dictionary data. , work? Instead requiring name-value pairs function call, translate functions look relevant name value pairs dictionary: translate_categories() replaces category levels labels, translate_names() replaces variable names labels. useful variable names become column values, e.g., using pivot_longer melt: translate_data() general convenience tool : sets variable labels column attributes recodes category levels corresponding labels converts characters factors (factors required apply category order specified dictionary) divide continuous variables corresponding modeling divisor `units = ‘model’, update labels accordingly
code: data_peng %>%    mutate(sex = translate_categories(sex, dictionary = dd_peng)) #> # A tibble: 344 × 5 #>    species sex             body_mass_g bill_length_mm bill_depth_mm #>    <fct>   <chr>                 <int>          <dbl>         <dbl> #>  1 Adelie  Male penguins          3750           39.1          18.7 #>  2 Adelie  Female penguins        3800           39.5          17.4 #>  3 Adelie  Female penguins        3250           40.3          18   #>  4 Adelie  <NA>                     NA           NA            NA   #>  5 Adelie  Female penguins        3450           36.7          19.3 #>  6 Adelie  Male penguins          3650           39.3          20.6 #>  7 Adelie  Female penguins        3625           38.9          17.8 #>  8 Adelie  Male penguins          4675           39.2          19.6 #>  9 Adelie  <NA>                   3475           34.1          18.1 #> 10 Adelie  <NA>                   4250           42            20.2 #> # ℹ 334 more rows data_peng %>%    pivot_longer(starts_with("bill_")) %>%    mutate(name = translate_names(name, dictionary = dd_peng)) #> # A tibble: 688 × 5 #>    species sex    body_mass_g name        value #>    <fct>   <fct>        <int> <chr>       <dbl> #>  1 Adelie  male          3750 Bill length  39.1 #>  2 Adelie  male          3750 Bill depth   18.7 #>  3 Adelie  female        3800 Bill length  39.5 #>  4 Adelie  female        3800 Bill depth   17.4 #>  5 Adelie  female        3250 Bill length  40.3 #>  6 Adelie  female        3250 Bill depth   18   #>  7 Adelie  <NA>            NA Bill length  NA   #>  8 Adelie  <NA>            NA Bill depth   NA   #>  9 Adelie  female        3450 Bill length  36.7 #> 10 Adelie  female        3450 Bill depth   19.3 #> # ℹ 678 more rows data_peng %>%    translate_data(dictionary = dd_peng) #> # A tibble: 344 × 5 #>    species sex             body_mass_g bill_length_mm bill_depth_mm #>    <fct>   <fct>                 <int>          <dbl>         <dbl> #>  1 Adelie  Male penguins          3750           39.1          18.7 #>  2 Adelie  Female penguins        3800           39.5          17.4 #>  3 Adelie  Female penguins        3250           40.3          18   #>  4 Adelie  <NA>                     NA           NA            NA   #>  5 Adelie  Female penguins        3450           36.7          19.3 #>  6 Adelie  Male penguins          3650           39.3          20.6 #>  7 Adelie  Female penguins        3625           38.9          17.8 #>  8 Adelie  Male penguins          4675           39.2          19.6 #>  9 Adelie  <NA>                   3475           34.1          18.1 #> 10 Adelie  <NA>                   4250           42            20.2 #> # ℹ 334 more rows

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: Writing meta data dictionary make easier put information outputs. However, supplying dictionary input every single perinary function gets unnecessarily tedious. convenience, can save dictionary make “default” dictionary perinary functions current R session. default dictionary used whenever perinary function called dictionary explicitly supplied user. Note: remainder ReadMe, omit dictionary = dd_peng perinary functions set default. However, take semantic shortcut may confusing readers skip sections copy/paste example code.
code: set_default_dictionary(dd_peng)  data_peng %>%    pivot_longer(starts_with("bill_")) %>%    mutate(name = translate_names(name),          sex = translate_categories(sex)) #> # A tibble: 688 × 5 #>    species sex             body_mass_g name        value #>    <fct>   <chr>                 <int> <chr>       <dbl> #>  1 Adelie  Male penguins          3750 Bill length  39.1 #>  2 Adelie  Male penguins          3750 Bill depth   18.7 #>  3 Adelie  Female penguins        3800 Bill length  39.5 #>  4 Adelie  Female penguins        3800 Bill depth   17.4 #>  5 Adelie  Female penguins        3250 Bill length  40.3 #>  6 Adelie  Female penguins        3250 Bill depth   18   #>  7 Adelie  <NA>                     NA Bill length  NA   #>  8 Adelie  <NA>                     NA Bill depth   NA   #>  9 Adelie  Female penguins        3450 Bill length  36.7 #> 10 Adelie  Female penguins        3450 Bill depth   19.3 #> # ℹ 678 more rows

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries
text: using package like gtsummary, incorporates variable labels automatically, translate() probably need perinary. rare cases, gtsummary may provide tools tabulate modeling results, circumstances recommend using broom::tidy followed index_terms set data tabulating. Let’s start tidied model output broom: use index_terms() attach variable category metadata dictionary re-order rows match dictionary order. helpful want variables listed consistent order throughout report. also earlier use set_variable_order() relevance — note variable ordering set reflected table (.e., species first). Third, tabulate results
code: library(broom)  fit <- data_peng %>%    translate_data(units = 'model', dictionary = dd_peng) %>%    lm(formula = body_mass_g ~ sex + species + bill_length_mm + bill_depth_mm,       data = .) %>%    tidy(conf.int = TRUE)  fit #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   term               estimate std.error statistic  p.value conf.low conf.high #>   <chr>                 <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 (Intercept)           1036.     482.       2.15 3.24e- 2     87.6     1984. #> 2 sexFemale penguins    -437.      49.1     -8.90 3.84e-17   -534.      -341. #> 3 speciesAdelie          245.      84.6      2.90 4.01e- 3     78.7      412. #> 4 speciesGentoo         1689.      82.2     20.5  8.99e-61   1527.      1850. #> 5 bill_length_mm         133.      36.2      3.66 2.90e- 4     61.4      204. #> 6 bill_depth_mm          440.     101.       4.35 1.83e- 5    241.       639. fit_sorted <- index_terms(fit, dictionary = dd_peng) library(flextable) #>  #> Attaching package: 'flextable' #> The following object is masked from 'package:purrr': #>  #>     compose library(table.glue)  data_ft <- fit_sorted %>%    filter(name != "(Intercept)") %>%    # push labels for continuous bill variables into the label column   mutate(     label = coalesce(label,                       translate_names(name, units = 'model',                                       dictionary = dd_peng)     )   ) %>%    # Add a custom group for the bill variables   add_count(name) %>%    mutate(name = if_else(n==1,                          "Bill dimensions",                          translate_names(name))) %>%    # drop unused columns and make final modifications   transmute(     name,      label,     # format results with table.glue     estimate = if_else(       reference,        true = "0 (reference)",        false = table_glue("{estimate} ({conf.low}, {conf.high})")),     p.value = table_pvalue(p.value)   )  data_ft %>%    as_grouped_data(groups = 'name') %>%    as_flextable(hide_grouplabel = TRUE) %>%    theme_box() %>%    set_header_labels(     label = "Characteristic",     estimate = "Estimated body mass\ndifference, grams (95% CI)",      p.value = "P-value"   ) %>%    autofit() %>%    padding(i = ~ is.na(name), j = 1, padding.left = 15) %>%    align(j = c(2, 3), align = 'center', part = 'all')

what: Append Model Output with Missing Terms — append_term_key
title: Append Model Output with Missing Terms — append_term_key
text: Add columns indicating variable name, category level, category label, reference groups model output (row also added reference group)

title: Append Model Output with Missing Terms — append_term_key
code: append_term_key(   data,   dictionary = NULL,   term_separator = "",   term_colname = "term" )

title: Append Model Output with Missing Terms — append_term_key
text: data data frame tibble containing model output, typically broom::tidy(). Must contain column identifies model terms. dictionary data dictionary object. NULL, uses default dictionary set via set_default_dictionary(). term_separator string used separate variable names category values constructing matching terms. Default "" match default separator R modeling functions use. term_colname Name column data contains term identifiers. Default "term".

title: Append Model Output with Missing Terms — append_term_key
text: tibble original data joined term key dictionary. result includes additional columns like variable category, ensures consistent ordering model terms appended reference rows (missing).

title: Append Model Output with Missing Terms — append_term_key
text: function uses term column (specified column) input data match term key derived dictionary. final output includes variable column preserves term order, appending reference terms (needed) completeness.

title: Internal assertion helpers — assert_helpers
text: Lightweight assertions used across perinary provide clear, actionable error warning messages: assert_valid_field(): error field valid variable type (e.g., category metadata numeric variables). assert_in_set(): error one values among allowed choices, readable list unknown recognized values. assert_valid_dotdot(): validate either ... .list used (). Warn empty. helpers intended end-users; exist keep error messages consistent informative within package.

title: Internal assertion helpers — assert_helpers
code: assert_valid_field(name, type, field, suggest = NULL)  assert_in_set(   values,   choices,   value_type = "values",   value_location = "dictionary",   variable = NULL )  assert_valid_dotdot(..., .list, names_required = TRUE)

title: Internal assertion helpers — assert_helpers
text: name Character scalar. Variable name validated. type Character scalar. Variable type readable noun (e.g., "numeric", "nominal", "identifier"). field Character scalar. metadata field supplied (e.g., "category_labels", "divby_modeling"). suggest Optional character scalar guidance user (e.g., "use set_category_labels()"). values Character vector user-supplied values check. choices Character vector allowed values. value_type Character scalar used messages describe values (default "values"). Set something specific like "category levels" helpful. variable Optional character scalar. provided, error message name variable associated failed check. ... Zero unquoted inputs (collected list caller). Intended mutually exclusive .list. .list Optional pre-assembled list inputs. Intended mutually exclusive ....

title: Internal assertion helpers — assert_helpers
text: assert_valid_field() returns NULL invisibly success; otherwise aborts informative message. assert_in_set() returns NULL invisibly values choices; otherwise aborts bulleted list unknown values recognized set. assert_valid_dotdot() returns NULL invisibly; aborts ... .list supplied, warns empty.

title: Internal assertion helpers — assert_helpers
text: Errors use cli::cli_abort() bulleted message. Messages reference variable names fields explicitly (e.g., `date_measure`). Helpers modify inputs; validate signal.

title: Bind Dictionaries Together — bind_dictionary
text: Binding dictionaries together can useful variables exist multiple datasets want re-write meta information datasets.

title: Bind Dictionaries Together — bind_dictionary
code: bind_dictionary(   ...,   .list = NULL,   conflict_preference = "left",   drop_unmatched_variables = FALSE )  left_bind(..., .list = NULL, drop_unmatched_variables = FALSE)  right_bind(..., .list = NULL, drop_unmatched_variables = FALSE)

title: Bind Dictionaries Together — bind_dictionary
text: ... Data dictionary objects combine. .list argument allows bind_dictionary used programmatically optional. intended used alternative ..... Specifically, instead bind_dictionary(x, y), can use bind_dictionary(.list = list(x, y)). helpful lot dictionaries want bind programmatically. conflict_preference character value indicating x y overlapping variables. "left", definitions x dictionary preferred x left y function arguments. "right", y definitions preferred. drop_unmatched_variables logical. TRUE, variables y x retained output. FALSE (default), variables x y overlap x included, subject conflict_preference.

title: Bind Dictionaries Together — bind_dictionary
text: data dictionary object

title: Bind Dictionaries Together — bind_dictionary
code: dd_age_years <- data_dictionary(   numeric_variable(     name = "age_years",     label = "Age",     units = 'years',     divby_modeling = 10   ) )  dd_age_group <- data_dictionary(   nominal_variable(     name = "age_group",     label = "Age group",     description = "Ages of 0 to < 50, 50 to < 60, and \u2265 60 years",     category_levels = c("age_lt_50", "age_gteq_50_lt_60", "age_gteq_60"),     category_labels = c("0 to < 50", "50 to < 60", "\u2265 60")   ) )  bind_dictionary(dd_age_years, dd_age_group) #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   name      type    label     description   units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr>     <chr>   <chr>     <chr>         <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 age_years Numeric Age       none          years 10             none            #> 2 age_group Nominal Age group Ages of 0 to… none  none           age_lt_50, age… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>

code: age_years <- numeric_variable(   name = "age",   label = "Age of participant",   units = "years" )  gender <- nominal_variable(   name = "gender",   label = "Gender of participant",   category_levels = c("M", "F"),   category_labels = c("Male", "Female") )  dd <- data_dictionary(age_years, gender) print(dd) #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   name   type    label          description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr>  <chr>   <chr>          <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 age    Numeric Age of partic… none        years none           none            #> 2 gender Nominal Gender of par… none        none  none           M and F         #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>  dd <- data_dictionary(.list = list(age_years, gender)) print(dd) #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>   name   type    label          description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr>  <chr>   <chr>          <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 age    Numeric Age of partic… none        years none           none            #> 2 gender Nominal Gender of par… none        none  none           M and F         #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>

title: Get Acronym Definitions — get_acronym_defs

title: Get Acronym Definitions — get_acronym_defs
code: get_acronym_defs(   dictionary,   acronyms = NULL,   sep = "; ",   sep_last = NULL,   show_warnings = TRUE )

title: Get Acronym Definitions — get_acronym_defs
text: dictionary data dictionary object acronyms NULL character vector acronyms. One names(dictionary$get_acronyms()). NULL, acronyms dictionary selected. sep character; separator acronyms. sep_last NULL character; separator last two acronyms. NULL, sep used instead. show_warnings logical; whether display warnings.

title: Get Acronym Definitions — get_acronym_defs
text: character string form "acronym1 = acronym 1 description; acronym2 = acronym 2 description."

title: Get Acronym Definitions — get_acronym_defs
text: Tyler Sagendorf

title: Get Acronym Definitions — get_acronym_defs
code: dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) dd$get_acronyms() # NULL #> NULL  dd <- set_acronyms(dictionary = dd,                    BP = "blood pressure",                    SBP = "systolic blood pressure",                    DBP = "diastolic blood pressure")  dd$get_acronyms() #>                         BP                        DBP  #>           "blood pressure" "diastolic blood pressure"  #>                        SBP  #>  "systolic blood pressure"   get_acronym_defs(dd, acronyms = NULL) # use all acronyms (default) #> [1] "BP = blood pressure; DBP = diastolic blood pressure; SBP = systolic blood pressure." get_acronym_defs(dd, acronyms = c("DBP", "BP")) #> [1] "BP = blood pressure; DBP = diastolic blood pressure."  # Attempt to select an acronym that was not set get_acronym_defs(dd, acronyms = "FOO") # character(0L) #> Warning: No acronyms match `names(dictionary$get_acronyms())`. #> character(0)

what: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
title: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
text: Returns tibble dictionary contents, optionally formatting missing values nominal variable categories. can useful custom workflows require dictionary information tabular form.

title: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
code: get_dictionary(   dictionary,   format_missing = FALSE,   format_categories = FALSE,   as_code = FALSE )

title: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
text: dictionary data dictionary object format_missing Logical. TRUE, missing metadata fields (like labels units) returned stored dictionary. FALSE (default), missing values replaced NA. format_categories Logical. TRUE, nominal category information returned exactly stored dictionary. FALSE (default), category levels labels returned list-columns extracted variable objects. as_code Logical. TRUE, text returned code can copy/pasted R script construct dictionary. FALSE (default), unknowns returned tibble.

title: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
text: tibble one row per variable (as_code = FALSE), containing meta data fields label, description, units, divby_modeling, category_levels, category_labels. as_code = TRUE, string containing code construct original dictionary.

title: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
text: Byron Jaeger, Tyler Sagendorf

title: Extract Dictionary Meta Data as a Tibble — get_dictionary
code: dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) get_dictionary(dd) #> # A tibble: 5 × 7 #>   name    label description units divby_modeling category_levels category_labels #>   <chr>   <chr> <chr>       <chr>          <dbl> <named list>    <named list>    #> 1 Sepal.… NA    NA          NA                NA <NULL>          <NULL>          #> 2 Sepal.… NA    NA          NA                NA <NULL>          <NULL>          #> 3 Petal.… NA    NA          NA                NA <NULL>          <NULL>          #> 4 Petal.… NA    NA          NA                NA <NULL>          <NULL>          #> 5 Species NA    NA          NA                NA <chr [3]>       <chr [3]>        # Code to recreate dd dd_code <- get_dictionary(dd, as_code = TRUE) dd_code #> [1] "data_dictionary(.list = list(numeric_variable(name='Sepal.Length',label=NULL,description=NULL,units=NULL,divby_modeling=NULL),numeric_variable(name='Sepal.Width',label=NULL,description=NULL,units=NULL,divby_modeling=NULL),numeric_variable(name='Petal.Length',label=NULL,description=NULL,units=NULL,divby_modeling=NULL),numeric_variable(name='Petal.Width',label=NULL,description=NULL,units=NULL,divby_modeling=NULL),nominal_variable(name='Species',label=NULL,description=NULL,category_levels=c('setosa','versicolor','virginica'),category_labels=c('setosa','versicolor','virginica'))))"  # Create data dictionary from code string dd_new <- eval(parse(text = dd_code)) all.equal(dd, dd_new) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

what: Get Term Key for Nominal Variables — get_term_key
title: Get Term Key for Nominal Variables — get_term_key
text: Returns tibble linking level label nominal variable modeling term, using specified separator variable name category value. especially useful joining dictionary information model output, coefficients terms regression tables.

title: Get Term Key for Nominal Variables — get_term_key
code: get_term_key(   dictionary,   adjust_to = NULL,   term_separator = "",   term_colname = "term" )

title: Get Term Key for Nominal Variables — get_term_key
text: dictionary data dictionary object adjust_to Optional. tibble includes column term names (default: 'term'). supplied, output filtered retain terms found adjust_to match reference level. term_separator string used separate variable name category value output term column. Default "". term_colname character string giving column name use terms column returned tibble. Default "term".

title: Get Term Key for Nominal Variables — get_term_key
text: tibble one row per variable-category combination columns variable, category type (levels labels), term. adjust_to provided, tibble filtered accordingly includes category column.

title: Get Term Key for Nominal Variables — get_term_key
code: dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) get_term_key(dd) #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   name    level      label      reference category_type term              #>   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>      <lgl>     <chr>         <chr>             #> 1 Species setosa     setosa     TRUE      levels        Speciessetosa     #> 2 Species versicolor versicolor FALSE     levels        Speciesversicolor #> 3 Species virginica  virginica  FALSE     levels        Speciesvirginica

what: Get Unknown Fields in Dictionary — get_unknowns
title: Get Unknown Fields in Dictionary — get_unknowns
text: Get Unknown Fields Dictionary

title: Get Unknown Fields in Dictionary — get_unknowns
code: get_unknowns(   dictionary = NULL,   as_request = FALSE,   as_code = FALSE,   show_optional = FALSE )

title: Get Unknown Fields in Dictionary — get_unknowns
text: dictionary data dictionary object as_request logical. TRUE, text returned readable format can pasted settings, e.g., e-mail requesting unknown information. FALSE (default), unknowns returned tibble. as_code logical. TRUE, text returned code can copy/pasted R script. FALSE (default), unknowns returned tibble. show_optional logical. TRUE, unknowns include possible fields variables dictionary. FALSE (default), labels units presented.

title: Get Unknown Fields in Dictionary — get_unknowns
text: as_request = FALSE as_code = FALSE, tibble returned. as_request = TRUE as_code = TRUE, text returned.

title: Get Unknown Fields in Dictionary — get_unknowns
code: # units isn't returned in the output because it is specified (known) get_unknowns(data_dictionary(numeric_variable("a", units = 'years'))) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   variable type    name  value #>   <chr>    <chr>   <fct> <chr> #> 1 a        Numeric label none   # label isn't returned in the output because it is specified (known) get_unknowns(data_dictionary(nominal_variable("b", label = 'example'))) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   variable type    name            value #>   <chr>    <chr>   <fct>           <chr> #> 1 b        Nominal category_labels none  dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) |>   set_labels(Sepal.Length = "Sepal length",             Sepal.Width = "Sepal width",             Species = "Flower species") |>   set_descriptions(Species = "The species are all subtypes of iris") |>   set_units(Sepal.Length = "cm")  # as_request = TRUE returns this information in a shareable format get_unknowns(dd, as_request = TRUE) #> A label to use for this variable in reports: #>  #>   - Petal.Length = ? #>   - Petal.Width = ? #>  #> Category labels for this variable (labels are shown in reports): #>  #>   - Species: setosa = ?;  versicolor = ?;  virginica = ? #>  #> Variable units (e.g., age in years): #>  #>   - Sepal.Width = ? #>   - Petal.Length = ? #>   - Petal.Width = ?   # as_code = TRUE gives a head start on filling in the missing info get_unknowns(dd, as_code = TRUE) #> set_labels(Petal.Length  = "", #>            Petal.Width  = "") |>  #> set_category_labels(Species = c(setosa = "", #>                                 versicolor = "", #>                                 virginica = "")) |>  #> set_units(Sepal.Width  = "", #>           Petal.Length  = "", #>           Petal.Width  = "")

what: Order columns by dictionary variables — index_columns
title: Order columns by dictionary variables — index_columns
text: Reorders columns data frame columns whose names appear DataDictionary come first, order defined dictionary. Columns present dictionary optionally kept end.

title: Order columns by dictionary variables — index_columns
code: index_columns(data, dictionary = NULL, keep_unmatched = TRUE)

title: Order columns by dictionary variables — index_columns
text: data data frame tibble whose columns reordered. dictionary data dictionary object. NULL, uses default dictionary set via set_default_dictionary(). keep_unmatched Logical. TRUE (default), columns data defined dictionary retained appended matched columns. FALSE, columns dropped output.

title: Order columns by dictionary variables — index_columns
text: type data, columns reordered (optionally filtered) according dictionary.

title: Order columns by dictionary variables — index_columns
code: dd <- data_dictionary(   numeric_variable("bill_length_mm", label = "Bill length", units = "mm"),   numeric_variable("bill_depth_mm",  label = "Bill depth",  units = "mm"),   nominal_variable("species", label = "Species",                    category_levels = c("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo")) )  df <- data.frame(   species        = c("Adelie", "Chinstrap"),   extra_col      = 1:2,   bill_depth_mm  = c(18.7, 17.4),   bill_length_mm = c(39.1, 46.5) )  # Matched columns appear in dictionary order; extra_col is appended index_columns(df, dictionary = dd) #>   bill_length_mm bill_depth_mm   species extra_col #> 1           39.1          18.7    Adelie         1 #> 2           46.5          17.4 Chinstrap         2  # Drop columns not in the dictionary index_columns(df, dictionary = dd, keep_unmatched = FALSE) #>   bill_length_mm bill_depth_mm   species #> 1           39.1          18.7    Adelie #> 2           46.5          17.4 Chinstrap

what: Order rows by dictionary terms — index_rows
title: Order rows by dictionary terms — index_rows
text: Reorders data frame rows associated nominal variables follow order defined DataDictionary. useful want multiple outputs present variables consistent order.

title: Order rows by dictionary terms — index_rows
code: index_rows(data, dictionary = NULL, names = "name", levels = "level")

title: Order rows by dictionary terms — index_rows
text: data data frame tibble containing rows order. Must include columns identify variable name category level. dictionary data dictionary object. NULL, uses default dictionary set via set_default_dictionary(). names Character value giving column name stores variable name data. Default "name". levels Character value giving column name stores category code/level data. Default "level".

title: Order rows by dictionary terms — index_rows
text: type data, rows re-ordered match order variables dictionary , within nominal variables, order categories.

title: Order rows by dictionary terms — index_rows
code: dd <- data_dictionary(   nominal_variable(     "gender",     label = "Gender",     category_levels = c("M", "F"),     category_labels = c("Male", "Female")   ) )  df <- tibble::tibble(   name = c("gender", "gender"),   level = c("F", "M"),   n = c(12, 18) )  index_rows(df, dictionary = dd) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   name   level     n #>   <chr>  <chr> <dbl> #> 1 gender M        18 #> 2 gender F        12

what: Order model terms by dictionary variables and category levels — index_terms
title: Order model terms by dictionary variables and category levels — index_terms
text: convenience wrapper broom::tidy() output attaches variable category metadata dictionary, sorts rows dictionary order via index_rows().

title: Order model terms by dictionary variables and category levels — index_terms
code: index_terms(   data,   dictionary = NULL,   term_separator = "",   term_colname = "term",   names = "name",   levels = "level" )

title: Order model terms by dictionary variables and category levels — index_terms
text: data data frame model output, typically broom::tidy(). Must contain column identifying model terms (see term_colname). dictionary data dictionary object. NULL, uses default dictionary set via set_default_dictionary(). term_separator string used separate variable names category values constructing matching terms. Default "" match default separator R modeling functions use. term_colname Name column data contains term identifiers. Default "term". names Character value giving column name stores variable name enriched output. Default "name". levels Character value giving column name stores category code/level enriched output. Default "level".

title: Order model terms by dictionary variables and category levels — index_terms
text: type data (term-key columns appended), rows sorted match variable category order defined dictionary.

title: Order model terms by dictionary variables and category levels — index_terms
code: library(broom)  dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) |>   set_variable_order(Species, .before = 1)  fit <- tidy(lm(Sepal.Length ~ ., data = iris))  index_terms(fit, dictionary = dd) #> # A tibble: 7 × 9 #>   name        level label reference term  estimate std.error statistic   p.value #>   <chr>       <chr> <chr> <lgl>     <chr>    <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Species     seto… seto… TRUE      Spec…   NA       NA          NA    NA        #> 2 Species     vers… vers… FALSE     Spec…   -0.724    0.240      -3.01  3.06e- 3 #> 3 Species     virg… virg… FALSE     Spec…   -1.02     0.334      -3.07  2.58e- 3 #> 4 Sepal.Width NA    NA    FALSE     Sepa…    0.496    0.0861      5.76  4.87e- 8 #> 5 Petal.Leng… NA    NA    FALSE     Peta…    0.829    0.0685     12.1   1.07e-23 #> 6 Petal.Width NA    NA    FALSE     Peta…   -0.315    0.151      -2.08  3.89e- 2 #> 7 (Intercept) NA    NA    FALSE     (Int…    2.17     0.280       7.76  1.43e-12

title: Internal inference helpers — infer_helpers
text: Small utilities used across perinary standardize inputs: infer_dotdot(): Canonicalizes .../.list single list. infer_meta(): Resolves DataDictionary use (argument package default) validates class. infer_overlapping_variables(): Finds variables present data dictionary (optionally warning unmatched columns). exported documented maintainers.

title: Internal inference helpers — infer_helpers
code: infer_dotdot(..., .list = NULL)  infer_templates(.dots, dictionary, field)  infer_curlies(x)  infer_meta(dictionary)  infer_overlapping_variables(dictionary, data, warn_unmatched)

title: Internal inference helpers — infer_helpers
text: ... Name–value pairs objects collect list. .list pre-assembled list. supplied non-NULL, takes precedence .... .dots list name value pairs. Values type environment captured translated regular strings based existing templates dictionary dictionary DataDictionary NULL. NULL, function attempts use package default set via set_default_dictionary(). field designate whether looking "description" field "label" field. x character string data data frame data.table whose column names checked dictionary. warn_unmatched Logical. TRUE, emit single warning listing variables present data absent dictionary.

title: Internal inference helpers — infer_helpers
text: infer_dotdot() returns list. .list non-NULL, returned -; otherwise list(...) returned. character vector symbols curly braces: infer_meta() returns validated DataDictionary. infer_overlapping_variables() returns character vector column names present data dictionary.

title: Internal inference helpers — infer_helpers
text: infer_meta() resolves effective dictionary use preferring provided dictionary falling back package default stored .perinary_internal$dictionary. neither available, error thrown guidance creating (as_data_dictionary()) setting (set_default_dictionary()) dictionary. “Unmatched” variables names(data) dictionary define (per dictionary$get_names()). warn_unmatched TRUE, function warns concise list guidance suppressing warning.

title: Internal inference helpers — infer_helpers
text: Functions never modify inputs. Errors use cli::cli_abort() actionable guidance. Variable names messages shown explicitly (e.g., date_measure).

title: Internal paste helpers — paste_helpers
text: Small utilities format vectors/lists readable messages logs. Used throughout perinary make assertion warning text concise human-friendly. paste_collapse(): collapse vector commas natural language conjunction last element (e.g., ", b, c"). paste_named_vec(): format (named) vector list "name = value" pairs (lists rendered name = c(v1, v2, ...)), filling missing/empty names sentinel.

title: Internal paste helpers — paste_helpers
code: paste_collapse(x, as_code = FALSE)  paste_named_vec(x, name_fill = "MISSING_NAME")

title: Internal paste helpers — paste_helpers
text: x vector (character, numeric, etc.) collapse. NULL returns "none". Length-1 values coerced character returned unchanged. name_fill Character scalar used replace missing empty names formatting (default "MISSING_NAME").

title: Internal paste helpers — paste_helpers
text: paste_collapse() returns single character scalar elements x collapsed ", " natural "" last item. paste_named_vec() returns: x list: named character vector, element form "name = c(v1, v2, ...)". element name corresponds original list element name (name_fill missing/empty). x atomic vector: single character scalar form "name1 = value1, name2 = value2, ...".

title: Internal paste helpers — paste_helpers
text: NULL inputs rendered "none" avoid noisy NULL prints. last separator " " length 2, otherwise ", ". Empty missing names replaced name_fill (default "MISSING_NAME").

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries — perinary-package
text: Keeps labels relevant information variables lightweight class can applied datasets various ways, aiming eliminate redundancies promote readable, maintainable code.

title: perinary: Data Dictionaries — perinary-package
text: Maintainer: Byron Jaeger bcjaeger@perisphere-rwe.com (ORCID) contributors: Tyler Sagendorf [contributor]

title: Set and Remove Acronyms — set_acronyms
text: Add acronyms descriptions DataDictionary. Acronyms can also removed longer used.

title: Set and Remove Acronyms — set_acronyms
code: set_acronyms(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL, show_warnings = TRUE)

title: Set and Remove Acronyms — set_acronyms
text: dictionary data dictionary object ... one comma-separated name-value pairs. Pairs form acronym = "spelled-acronym" "acronym" = "spelled-acronym". .list list name-value pairs. argument optional intended used place ... programmatic setting acronyms. show_warnings logical; whether display warnings.

title: Set and Remove Acronyms — set_acronyms
text: modified dictionary.

title: Set and Remove Acronyms — set_acronyms
code: dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) dd$get_acronyms() # NULL #> NULL  dd <- set_acronyms(dictionary = dd,                    SBP = "systolic blood pressure",                    DBP = "diastolic blood pressure")  dd$get_acronyms() #>                        DBP                        SBP  #> "diastolic blood pressure"  "systolic blood pressure"

what: Set the default dictionary used by perinary functions — set_default_dictionary
title: Set the default dictionary used by perinary functions — set_default_dictionary
text: Set default dictionary used perinary functions

title: Set the default dictionary used by perinary functions — set_default_dictionary
code: set_default_dictionary(dictionary)

title: Set the default dictionary used by perinary functions — set_default_dictionary
text: dictionary data dictionary object

title: Set Identifier Variables — set_identifiers
text: Set Identifier Variables

title: Set Identifier Variables — set_identifiers
code: set_identifiers(dictionary, ...)

title: Set Identifier Variables — set_identifiers
text: dictionary data dictionary object ... quoted unquoted names identifier variables

title: Set Identifier Variables — set_identifiers
text: modified dictionary

title: Modify Data Dictionary Elements — set_labels
text: Set metadata DataDictionary object.

title: Modify Data Dictionary Elements — set_labels
code: set_labels(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL)  set_descriptions(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL)  set_units(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL)  set_divby_modeling(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL)  set_category_labels(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL)  set_category_order(dictionary, ..., .list = NULL)

title: Modify Data Dictionary Elements — set_labels
text: dictionary data dictionary object ... Name-value pairs. name gives name variable modified. value must : character value set_labels(), set_units(), set_descriptions() numeric value set_divby_modeling() character vector set_category_levels() set_category_labels() .list list name-value pairs. argument optional intended used place ... programmatic setting meta data.

title: Modify Data Dictionary Elements — set_labels
text: modified dictionary

title: Modify Data Dictionary Elements — set_labels
code: dd <- as_data_dictionary(data.frame(a = 1, b = "cat", id = 1)) |>  set_identifiers(id) |>  set_labels(a = "numeric example", b = "categorical example") |>  set_units(a = "years") |>  set_divby_modeling(a = 10) |>  set_descriptions(a = "A variable used for examples") |>  set_category_labels(b = c("cat" = "A small lion"))  dd #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   name  type       label        description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr> <chr>      <chr>        <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 a     Numeric    numeric exa… A variable… years 10             none            #> 2 b     Nominal    categorical… none        none  none           cat             #> 3 id    Identifier none         none        none  none           none            #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>  # programmatic assignment  label_list <- list(a = "Listed numeric example",                    b = "Listed categorical example") set_labels(dd, .list = label_list) #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   name  type       label        description units divby_modeling category_levels #>   <chr> <chr>      <chr>        <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>           #> 1 a     Numeric    Listed nume… A variable… years 10             none            #> 2 b     Nominal    Listed cate… none        none  none           cat             #> 3 id    Identifier none         none        none  none           none            #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>

what: Set Label and Description Templates — set_templates
title: Set Label and Description Templates — set_templates
text: Add label description templates DataDictionary. Templates allow store re-use text placeholders, making easier describe multiple variables similar labels /descriptions (see examples).

title: Set Label and Description Templates — set_templates
code: set_label_templates(dictionary, ..., show_warnings = TRUE)  set_description_templates(dictionary, ..., show_warnings = TRUE)

title: Set Label and Description Templates — set_templates
text: dictionary data dictionary object ... one formulas. See details. show_warnings logical; whether display warnings.

title: Set Label and Description Templates — set_templates
text: modified dictionary.

title: Set Label and Description Templates — set_templates
code: library(perinary)  dd <- as_data_dictionary(iris) dd #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name         type    label description units divby_modeling category_levels    #>   <chr>        <chr>   <chr> <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>              #> 1 Sepal.Length Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 2 Sepal.Width  Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 3 Petal.Length Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 4 Petal.Width  Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 5 Species      Nominal none  none        none  none           setosa, versicolo… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>  dd <- set_label_templates(   dictionary = dd,   # dimension will be either "length" or "width"   contains("Sepal") ~ "Sepal {dimension}.",   contains("Petal") ~ "Petal {dimension}." )  dd # the label column has changed #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name         type    label description units divby_modeling category_levels    #>   <chr>        <chr>   <chr> <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>              #> 1 Sepal.Length Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 2 Sepal.Width  Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 3 Petal.Length Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 4 Petal.Width  Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 5 Species      Nominal none  none        none  none           setosa, versicolo… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>  dd <- set_description_templates(   dictionary = dd,   # dimension will be either "length" or "width"   contains("Sepal") ~ "The {dimension} of the sepal, in centimeters.",   contains("Petal") ~ "The {dimension} of the petal, in centimeters." )  dd # the description column has changed #> Data Dictionary: #> # A tibble: 5 × 8 #>   name         type    label description units divby_modeling category_levels    #>   <chr>        <chr>   <chr> <chr>       <chr> <chr>          <chr>              #> 1 Sepal.Length Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 2 Sepal.Width  Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 3 Petal.Length Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 4 Petal.Width  Numeric none  none        none  none           none               #> 5 Species      Nominal none  none        none  none           setosa, versicolo… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: category_labels <chr>

what: Modify order of variables in a data dictionary — set_variable_order
title: Modify order of variables in a data dictionary — set_variable_order
text: thin wrapper using dplyr::relocate set order variables data dictionary object. inputs (apart dictionary) named interpreted exactly dplyr::relocate. variables reordered specified dictionary returned.

title: Modify order of variables in a data dictionary — set_variable_order
code: set_variable_order(dictionary, ..., .before = NULL, .after = NULL)

title: Modify order of variables in a data dictionary — set_variable_order
text: dictionary data dictionary object ..., ., .see relocate documentation

title: Modify order of variables in a data dictionary — set_variable_order
text: order variables dictionary determines order results indexing functions applied, index_terms. Setting order variables match expected order presentation tables can help streamline table generation (see examples.)

title: Modify order of variables in a data dictionary — set_variable_order
code: dd_ordered <- as_data_dictionary(iris) |>   set_variable_order(Species, .before = Sepal.Length) |>   set_variable_order(ends_with("Length"), .after = Petal.Width)  set_default_dictionary(dd_ordered)  # index_terms() is using the order of variables in `dd_ordered` to # return a dataframe sorted the same way as `dd_ordered` is. Note # this also respects the ordering of categories within variables, # which is not straightforward to do with the usual dplyr::arrange()  lm(Sepal.Length ~ ., data = iris) |>   broom::tidy() |>   dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") |>   index_terms() #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>   name        level label reference term  estimate std.error statistic   p.value #>   <chr>       <chr> <chr> <lgl>     <chr>    <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Species     seto… seto… TRUE      Spec…   NA       NA          NA    NA        #> 2 Species     vers… vers… FALSE     Spec…   -0.724    0.240      -3.01  3.06e- 3 #> 3 Species     virg… virg… FALSE     Spec…   -1.02     0.334      -3.07  2.58e- 3 #> 4 Sepal.Width NA    NA    FALSE     Sepa…    0.496    0.0861      5.76  4.87e- 8 #> 5 Petal.Width NA    NA    FALSE     Peta…   -0.315    0.151      -2.08  3.89e- 2 #> 6 Petal.Leng… NA    NA    FALSE     Peta…    0.829    0.0685     12.1   1.07e-23

what: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
title: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
text: translate_*() helpers look metadata dictionary apply data: translate_data() attaches variable labels column attributes, optionally recodes category labels enforces category order, optionally scales numeric variables modeling. translate_names() replaces variable names variable labels. translate_categories() replaces category levels category labels enforces category order specified dictionary.

title: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
code: translate_data(   x,   ...,   dictionary = NULL,   units = "none",   warn_unmatched = TRUE,   apply_variable_labels = TRUE,   apply_category_labels = TRUE,   nominals_to_factor = TRUE,   drop_unused_levels = FALSE )  translate_names(   x,   ...,   .list = NULL,   dictionary = NULL,   units = "none",   to_factor = FALSE,   warn_unmatched = TRUE,   allow_duplicates = FALSE,   drop_unused_levels = FALSE )  translate_categories(   x,   ...,   .list = NULL,   dictionary = NULL,   names = NULL,   to_factor = FALSE,   warn_unmatched = TRUE,   drop_unused_levels = FALSE )

title: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
text: x object translate. translate_data(): x must data frame. translate_names() translate_categories(): x must character/factor. ... Additional arguments passed translate method. translate_data, ... uses tidyselect choose columns translate. tidyselect expression accepted (e.g., starts_with("age"), c(sex, bmi)). ... empty default everything(), .e. columns x considered translation. translate_names, ... must contain name-value pairs. name can value x corresponding value indicates translate x . can used  overrule value dictionary translate , add extra translation maps cases dictionary contain map values x (see examples). dictionary data dictionary object. NULL, uses default dictionary set via set_default_dictionary(). units character value indicating units incorporated variable labels. Valid choices "none" : include units labels, e.g., "Age" "descriptive" : include labels, e.g., "Age, years" "model" : include label model divisor, e.g., "Age, per 10 years" Note setting units = "model" modify values numeric variables data divby_modeling value. See details explanation. warn_unmatched logical value. TRUE (default), warning thrown whenever 1 variables data supporting documentation dictionary. FALSE, information presented warning. apply_variable_labels Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, variable labels attached column attributes. apply_category_labels Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, category levels replaced labels. Output also coerced factor order categories dictionary applied. nominals_to_factor Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, nominal variables converted factors level order determined using relevant information dictionary. drop_unused_levels Logical (default FALSE). TRUE (nominals_to_factor = TRUE to_factor = TRUE), unused levels dropped nominal variables. .list translate_names(): optional named character vector mapping old_name = "Label". supplied, takes precedence dictionary lookup (useful ad-hoc overrides). to_factor Logical (default TRUE). TRUE, output converted factor level order determined using relevant information dictionary. allow_duplicates logical value indicating whether allow single label attached multiple variable names. FALSE default allowing duplicate labels can cause unexpected errors downstream applications. Use caution. names translate_categories(): optional character vector variable names translate (useful x data frame disambiguation needed).

title: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
text: translate_data(): returns data frame labels/levels updated. Numeric columns may scaled units = "model". translate_names(): returns factor variable labels corresponding input names. translate_categories(): returns factor category labels corresponding input levels.

title: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
text: Transfer variable labels/units category labels/order vector data frame. choice units = "model", numeric variable dictionary divby_modeling value modified data, specifically divided divby_modeling. transform also indicated label said variable. E.g., variable age label "Age", unit "years" divby_modeling 10, setting units = "model" cause age column divided 10 label "Age, per 10 years"

title: Translate data, names, and categories with a dictionary — translate_data
code: age_years <- numeric_variable(   name = "age_years",   label = "Age of participant",   units = "years",   divby_modeling = 10 )  gender <- nominal_variable(   name = "gender",   label = "Gender of participant",   category_levels = c("M", "F"),   category_labels = c("Male", "Female") )  dd <- data_dictionary(age_years, gender) dat <- data.frame(age_years = 55, gender = "M")  # Translate an entire data frame (labels + categories; descriptive units) out1 <- translate_data(   dat, dictionary = dd,   units = "descriptive" ) purrr::map(out1, ~ attr(.x, "label")) #> $age_years #> [1] "Age of participant, years" #>  #> $gender #> [1] "Gender of participant" #>   # Prepare for modeling: scale numerics by divby_modeling and update labels out2 <- translate_data(   dat, dictionary = dd,   units = "model" ) out2$age_years # now 5.5 (i.e., 55 / 10) #> [1] 5.5 #> attr(,"label") #> [1] "Age of participant, per 10 years"  attr(out2$age_years, "label") # "Age of participant, per 10 years" #> [1] "Age of participant, per 10 years"  # Translate only the age_years column (gender left unchanged) out3 <- translate_data(   dat, dictionary = dd,   age_years ) attr(out3$age_years, "label") # "Age of participant" #> [1] "Age of participant" attr(out3$gender, "label")    # NULL #> NULL  # Replace variable names with labels (useful after pivot_longer) translate_names(c("age_years", "gender"), dictionary = dd) #> [1] "Age of participant"    "Gender of participant"  # Replace category codes with labels (and enforce order) translate_categories(dat$gender, dictionary = dd) #> [1] "Male"

what: Use a template to document a variable — use_template
title: Use a template to document a variable — use_template
text: function used inside set_labels() set_descriptions(). Calling use_template() functions allow plug values existing template, can set dictionary using set_label_templates

title: Use a template to document a variable — use_template
code: use_template(...)

title: Use a template to document a variable — use_template
text: ... name-value pairs. names must match variables dictionary template.

title: Use a template to document a variable — use_template
text: environment

title: Variable Constructors — variable_constructors
text: Constructors variable metadata classes.

title: Variable Constructors — variable_constructors
code: nominal_variable(   name,   label = NULL,   description = NULL,   category_levels = NULL,   category_labels = NULL )  numeric_variable(   name,   label = NULL,   description = NULL,   units = NULL,   divby_modeling = NULL )  date_variable(name, label = NULL, description = NULL, date_format = NULL)  identifier_variable(name, label = NULL, description = NULL)  logical_variable(   name,   label = NULL,   description = NULL,   category_labels = c("FALSE", "TRUE") )

title: Variable Constructors — variable_constructors
text: name Character. name variable. label Character NULL. short label variable. description Character NULL. longer description variable (optional). category_levels Character vector NULL. set unique category codes. category_labels Character vector NULL. Labels categories. nominal_variable, labels default category_levels provided. logical_variable, length 2 vector containing optional labels two levels: FALSE TRUE, respectively. units Character NULL. Units variable (e.g., "kg", "years"). divby_modeling Numeric NULL. constant indicating divide variable modeling. improves interpretability model coefficients. date_format Character NULL. Optional format string parsing date values (e.g., "%Y-%m-%d").

title: Variable Constructors — variable_constructors
text: DateVariable Calendar time-based data. IdentifierVariable ID variables, like subject IDs. LogicalVariable Indicator variables. NominalVariable Categorical data. NumericVariable Continuous quantitative data.

title: Variable Constructors — variable_constructors
code: # NominalVariable sex <- nominal_variable(   name = "sex",   label = "Sex",   description = "This is a longer description of the variable.",   category_levels = c("M", "F"),   category_labels = c("Male", "Female") ) sex #> Nominal Variable: #>   Name                 : sex  #>   Label                : Sex  #>   Description          : This is a longer description of the variable.  #>   Label template       : none  #>   Description template : none  #>   Category Levels      : M and F  #>   Category Labels      : Male and Female   # NumericVariable age <- numeric_variable(   name = "age",   label = "Age of participant",   units = "years",   # In a linear regression model, for example, the coefficient for age will   # be the change in the mean of the response per 10 year increase in age.   divby_modeling = 10 ) age #> Numeric Variable: #>   Name                 : age  #>   Label                : Age of participant  #>   Description          : none  #>   Label template       : none  #>   Description template : none  #>   Units                : years  #>   Modeling Divisor     : 10   # DateVariable visit_date <- date_variable(   name = "visit_date",   label = "Visit Date",   date_format = "%Y-%m-%d" ) visit_date #> Date Variable: #>   Name                 : visit_date  #>   Label                : Visit Date  #>   Description          : none  #>   Label template       : none  #>   Description template : none  #>   Date Format          : %Y-%m-%d   # IdentifierVariable patient_id <- identifier_variable(   name = "patient_id",   label = "Patient Identifier" ) patient_id #> Identifier Variable: #>   Name                 : patient_id  #>   Label                : Patient Identifier  #>   Description          : none  #>   Label template       : none  #>   Description template : none   # LogicalVariable smoker <- logical_variable(   name = "smoking_status",   label = "Current smoker",   # Labels correspond to FALSE and TRUE, respectively   category_labels = c("Non-smoker", "Smoker") ) smoker #> Logical Variable: #>   Name                 : smoking_status  #>   Label                : Current smoker  #>   Description          : none  #>   Label template       : none  #>   Description template : none  #>   Category Levels      : FALSE and TRUE  #>   Category Labels      : Non-smoker and Smoker
